blocks.body: Indefinite pronouns are words that refer to indefinite people, things, or places. Main groups: some- (someone, something), any- (anyone, anything), no- (nobody, nothing), every- (everyone, everything).

{ "title": "9. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "SOME- (someone, something, somewhere) — in statements and propositional questions.", "ANY- (anyone, anything, anywhere) — in questions, negatives (with not), or 'anyone' in statements.", "NO- (nobody, nothing, nowhere) - for negation (without not!", "EVERY- (everyone, everything, everywhere) - all without exception.", "All indefinite pronouns - grammatically singular (is, has, does).", "Adjectives come after the pronoun: something interesting.", "Can't: not + no- (double negative). Choose one!", "Someone/somebody, anyone/anybody are synonyms (-one is more formal)." ] }
